title: Universes (named sets of geo codes)
description: Lookup table for named sets of geo codes that indicator artifacts can $ref from `series_spec.expected_geographies` instead of inlining the array. Example: `all_states_and_uts` maps to the 36 ECI state codes; an indicator that covers all states/UTs can declare `{"$ref": "universes.json#/universes/all_states_and_uts"}` rather than re-listing 36 codes per artifact. Inline arrays remain allowed in `series_spec.expected_geographies` — this table is convenience for the common cases, not a gate. Keys are stable identifiers; values carry a description, the canonical geo code list, and the expected_count for consistency checks. Created for the folded-indicator model (TODO/20260517-folded-indicator-and-collection-inventory-handover.md §6.2).

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Validates `datasets/reference/in/universes.json`. Created for the folded-indicator expand phase (indicator.schema.json v1.6). Hand-authored reference data — `sources` is an empty array per CLAUDE.md §12 (the rationale lives in commit messages and any reference materials get attribution there).

sources.description: Per CLAUDE.md §12. This is hand-authored reference data; the canonical shape is an empty array. The commit message records the rationale and any reference materials consulted (delimitation history, Census year boundaries, ECI code list).

universes.description: Map from universe key (e.g. `all_states_and_uts`) to its definition. Keys MUST be lowercase snake_case and match `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` so indicator artifacts can $ref them stably.

description.description: Plain-English description of what this universe represents and why an indicator might pick it.

geo_codes.description: Canonical geo code list. Sort order is preserved verbatim; consumers MUST treat as a set.

expected_count.description: Must equal `geo_codes.length`. Carried explicitly as a consistency check (the validator asserts equality) so a code-list edit that accidentally drops or duplicates an entry fails Tier B.
